dialogue.utterance: Unit 4, respond for a 49-year-old woman with an allergic reaction at 1478 Third St.
Hi, I'm Matt and this is Daniel with the EMS team. What was the reason for calling 911 today?
I'm having really bad itching all over my body.
She started scratching her arms and legs shortly after eating tilapia and cabbage for dinner.
Ma'am, can you tell me your name?
My name is Elena.
Great. Can you tell us where you are and what day it is?
I'm at my Richmond apartment. It's January 31st, 2020.
Great. If you can squeeze my fingers, that would confirm your motor awareness.
Are you having any trouble speaking or breathing fully?
No, I can speak and breathe without issue.
Your breathing is not labored, and you have clear breath sounds bilaterally.
Respiratory rate is 20 breaths per minute at the moment.
No wheezing or stridor noted. Oxygen saturation is 96%.
Let's take your blood pressure now.
Your blood pressure is 156 over 90.
Your pulse is elevated, at 123.
Your radial pulse is strong and regular. No immediate threats to airway or breathing.
Skin is warm, dry, with hives present on arms and back.
You appear alert, aware, and oriented to four things. Can you confirm that?
Yes, I know where I am and what's going on.
Airway is patent and you are maintaining it yourself without difficulty.
Can you explain where the itching first started and has it changed?
It began shortly after I ate tilapia and cabbage; it's progressively spreading.
Have you had tilapia or cabbage before without issues?
Yes, multiple times, but nothing like this has happened before.
Do you have any known allergies?
No, no known drug or food allergies.
What medications do you take regularly?
Lisinopril, amlodipine, Vistaril, Prilosec, and Wellbutrin.
What's your past medical history?
I have high blood pressure, mild asthma, and used to have bronchitis.
When was your last meal and what did you eat?
20 minutes ago-I had tilapia and cabbage for dinner.
How would you describe the severity of your itching right now?
I'd say it's around a 6 or 7 on a scale of 10.
Is the itching confined to one area, or is it moving?
It's spread over my arms, legs, back, and neck.
Is it okay if we examine the hives on your neck and back?
Yes, go ahead.
I can see hives on your neck, upper arms, back, and palms. They've not responded to Benadryl.
I also notice redness on your scalp and neck.
Can you tell me if you're moving your arms and legs without difficulty?
She is moving all extremities well with full range of motion.
We need to place this on your chest to monitor your heart rhythm now.
EKG shows sinus tach at approximately 110-120.
Awareness and command are intact, which is reassuring.
I will now attach you to the cardiac monitor.
We placed you on a 4-lead ECG monitor showing sinus tach with no concern.
Is it okay with you if I insert an IV in your left arm?
Yes, go ahead.
IV successfully placed in your wrist using a 20G needle.
We are going to give you Benadryl to help control the itching. Okay with you?
Yes.
Administered 50mg Benadryl IV with a 10cc NS flush after two-provider cross-check.
You are showing signs of an allergic reaction-following protocol 3-3 for anaphylaxis.
We are also going to give you dexamethasone to help with the hives.
10mg dexamethasone IV administered, flushed with 10cc NS after a second cross-check.
Rechecked EKG-sticking with sinus tach at ~110-120.
We were unable to obtain a blood glucose at the scene.
Your breathing rate has dropped slightly to 16 breaths per minute.
Blood pressure has dropped to 132 over 71.
Heart rate is down to 113.
Oxygen saturation is now at 98%.
So far, no improvement. Hives are actually spreading on your neck.
Do you have any new symptoms or any worsening?
No new symptoms. The hives are only spreading but nothing else.
Rechecking heart rhythm just in case.
EKG still shows sinus tach. Still no arrhythmias detected.
Where would you like to be transported for further care?
Just take me to any nearby hospital.
Once ready, we'll use the five-point restraint system to ensure your safety during the ride.
We would prefer to keep you in a comfortable position during transport as you are presently.
The hospital is aware of the issue and will be watching the case closely while we are en route.
We are heading to Memorial Regional Medical Center, located a few minutes away.
Contact has been made with the receiving facility and they are aware of your findings.
We are now ready to handover to the emergency room with a full verbal and written report.
All findings include: no known allergies, hives spreading despite medications, hives on the neck area, skin is warm and dry, no new neurological or GI symptoms.
All interventions and medications were timed and documented. The IV was placed, meds given, and EKG was monitored. No distress, but also no improvement following initial treatment.
No adverse reactions noted. Vital signs trend in the right direction, with a steady baseline.
She has been placed on the stretcher comfortably.
Noted high gait stability; you will remain in the same standing position during transport if safe.
You will be admitted to ED27. The ER staff will continue to monitor and assess as appropriate.
Direct handoff includes: IV established, both Benadryl and Dexamethasone administered, EKG stable, no airway or breathing issues, hives spreading beyond initial examination.
No pain reported. All vitals within acceptable range. No acute distress. No visual symptoms or overt medical alert.
Now secured with five-point restraints and ready for transport.
The hospital has been notified. All medication and procedural events have been documented and will be reported upon arrival.
We are now en route with full monitoring equipment activated and no signs of changes.
All vitals remain stable at this time.
Monitoring per standard on all vitals-will update ER if changes occur.
You will be handed over to ED27 at Memorial Regional for further evaluation and treatment.
Transferred care of 49F with active allodynia and no current respiratory compromise, stable GCS 15.
All medications have been timed and documented as per protocol, with full notes on allergic reaction and IV placement.
No pain reported. No alert signs other than hives progressing. Vitals stable. Ongoing need for monitoring by ED team.
The transfer has been initiated and is now under the hospital's full care.
All documentation and logs are on file. Patient handed off with complete EMS summary as prior.
All logs and information have been shared as required. No complications during the transport.
